question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a malformed balance file that causes the parser to persist a partial batch of account balances (fewer than batchSize=200_000 entries) mid-transaction, leaving the Hashgraph balance history in an inconsistent state where some accounts show tampered balances and others show none?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted balance file with exactly batchSize=200_000 entries followed by a single tampered entry, exploiting the batch flush boundary in AccountBalanceFileParser to ensure the tampered entry is persisted in a separate transaction that bypasses integrity checks on the full snapshot, corrupting the historical balance record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file whose lines are each exactly fileBufferSize=200_000 bytes long, causing the BufferedReader to split lines at buffer boundaries, resulting in malformed account balance entries being silently skipped and producing an incomplete historical snapshot that misrepresents the true Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the constructor-time computation batch.setMaxItems(batchSize * 5L) = 1_000_000 by injecting a balance file with exactly 1_000_000 entries, causing the batch window to flush prematurely and persist an incomplete snapshot that omits legitimate accounts from the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed retry.setMaxAttempts(3) by repeatedly injecting a balance file that triggers a transient parse error on the third attempt, causing the parser to permanently skip the legitimate balance snapshot and leave a gap in the Hashgraph historical balance record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file that causes a parse exception on every retry attempt (up to maxAttempts=3), exhausting all retries and causing the parser to permanently drop the balance file, resulting in a missing historical snapshot that allows a prior tampered snapshot to remain as the last known Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transactionTimeout=5 minutes by injecting a balance file with a number of entries that takes just over 5 minutes to process, causing the database transaction to time out mid-batch, leaving a partial set of account balances persisted and corrupting the Hashgraph historical balance snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that is misidentified as StreamType.BALANCE by exploiting the type-detection logic, causing the balance parser to process a non-balance stream file and persist fabricated account balance entries into the Hashgraph historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.setQueueCapacity(1) setting by flooding the balance file queue with crafted files, causing legitimate balance files to be blocked from entering the queue and resulting in a stale or missing Hashgraph balance history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a balance file containing duplicate account entries that each individually pass validation but together exceed batchSize=200_000, causing the deduplication logic in AccountBalanceFileParser to silently drop legitimate entries and persist a tampered subset of the Hashgraph balance history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency=1s polling interval by injecting a crafted balance file at precisely the right moment to race with a legitimate file, causing the parser to process the tampered file first and persist false account balances as the authoritative Hashgraph history snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file with a null byte or special character at position fileBufferSize=200_000 in the stream, causing the BufferedReader to misparse the consensus timestamp header and persist a balance snapshot under a fabricated timestamp that misrepresents the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry backoff (minBackoff=250ms, maxBackoff=10s, multiplier=2) by crafting a balance file that fails on the first two attempts and succeeds on the third with tampered data, using the retry window to replace the legitimate file in the source location before the final attempt?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a balance file with zero valid entries (all lines malformed) so that batchPersister.persist() is called with an empty list, causing the AccountBalanceFile record to be saved with count=0 and no balance data, effectively erasing the Hashgraph balance history for that timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that batch.setMaxItems is computed from the default batchSize at construction time (not dynamically), so that if batchSize is later reconfigured to a smaller value, the maxItems ceiling remains at 1_000_000, allowing an attacker to inject a file with more items than expected and overflow the batch, corrupting the Hashgraph balance history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType.BALANCE type identifier to inject a synthetic balance file (synthetic=true flag) that bypasses signature verification, causing fabricated account balances to be persisted as part of the authoritative Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file that causes an OutOfMemoryError (which is excluded from retry via excludes=OutOfMemoryError.class) by providing a file with an extremely large number of token balance entries per account, causing the JVM to crash mid-parse and leaving a partial, corrupted Hashgraph balance snapshot in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that accountBalances and tokenBalances are flushed independently at batchSize=200_000 boundaries, by crafting a file where account balances flush at a different point than token balances, causing a mismatch between account and token balance records in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transactionTimeout=5 minutes combined with retry.maxAttempts=3 to cause a total of 15 minutes of transaction timeout retries, during which the balance parser is blocked from processing legitimate balance files, creating a gap in the Hashgraph historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a compressed balance file whose decompressed size far exceeds fileBufferSize=200_000, causing the parser to allocate an unexpectedly large buffer and exhaust heap memory, triggering an OutOfMemoryError that is excluded from retry and leaves the Hashgraph balance history in an incomplete state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency=1s polling interval to inject multiple crafted balance files in rapid succession, overwhelming the queueCapacity=1 queue and causing legitimate balance files to be dropped, resulting in gaps in the Hashgraph historical balance record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file where the first batchSize=200_000 entries are legitimate but subsequent entries contain tampered balances for high-value accounts, exploiting the batch flush mechanism to ensure the tampered entries are persisted in a separate database operation that may have weaker consistency guarantees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to inject environment variable overrides (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_BALANCE_BATCHSIZE) that reduce batchSize to 1, causing the parser to issue one database write per account balance entry and dramatically increasing the attack surface for transaction-level manipulation of the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set transactionTimeout to its minimum (1 second via @DurationMin(seconds=1)), causing nearly every balance file parse transaction to time out and roll back, preventing any legitimate Hashgraph balance history from being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set retry.maxAttempts to 0 (the @Min(0) allows zero), causing the parser to never retry failed balance file parses and permanently skip any balance file that encounters a transient error, leaving gaps in the Hashgraph historical record?
